exact_locator: Page 1, Legacy Acceptable Use Policy approved 3 September 2018, clause 3
risk_statement: MFA is optional for all staff, so a phished or reused password on a privileged account gives an attacker direct access to production systems and customer data.
why_it_applies: The gap concerns the strength of authentication required before access is granted. PR.AA-03 covers authenticating users commensurate with risk; A.8.5 covers secure authentication requirements.
evidence_needed: Entra ID Conditional Access export showing phishing-resistant MFA enforced for all privileged roles; confirmed at compliance-report.json by deny IDENTITY_MFA_WEAK for control-state.json#/identities/1

exact_locator: Page 2, Artifact reliability notes, third bullet (Entra ID configuration export)
risk_statement: MFA appears enabled but is not technically enforced for every privileged role, so the written rule would not prevent a non-administrator privileged account authenticating with a weak factor.
why_it_applies: Same control objective as G-001, but the failure is in enforcement configuration rather than policy text. Recorded separately because amending the document does not change the tenant.
evidence_needed: Post-implementation Conditional Access export reconciled against the privileged-role inventory
decision: Implement — closed by the same funded identity control

exact_locator: Page 1, Legacy AUP clause 6; page 2, Artifact reliability notes, second bullet
risk_statement: Production access is reviewed only on manager request, allowing privilege creep and orphaned credentials — a privileged service account with 418-day-old credentials and no owner is present in the current state.
why_it_applies: PR.AA-05 covers managing and reviewing access permissions; A.5.18 covers provisioning, review and removal of access rights on a defined cycle.
evidence_needed: Scheduled access-review records; JML automation output; confirmed at compliance-report.json by deny IDENTITY_OWNER_MISSING for control-state.json#/identities/2
decision: Implement — enforced by the funded identity control plus Engineering's automated joiner/mover/leaver review

exact_locator: Page 1, Legacy AUP clause 2; Stakeholder message: Legal, General Counsel, 10 July 2026
risk_statement: Unbounded inspection of personal devices without informed monitoring terms risks unlawful processing of employee personal data under the NDPA and produces evidence that may be unusable in incident response.
why_it_applies: The deficiency is a governance policy that authorises monitoring without a lawful, proportionate and disclosed basis. A.5.34 addresses privacy and protection of PII, which is the subject of Legal's concern; A.8.12 does not address lawfulness of monitoring and is mapped instead at G-005 where it applies.
evidence_needed: Updated BYOD clause; employee notification records; General Counsel approval records
decision: Reduce — addendum Clause 4 sets an interim monitoring boundary; full BYOD programme deferred (owner: General Counsel and Head of Security; trigger: next policy cycle, NDPC guidance update, or any BYOD incident)

exact_locator: Stakeholder message: Legal, 10 July 2026; absence of any data-handling clause across Legacy AUP clauses 1-8
risk_statement: Support attachments containing identity documents have no required classification, encryption or logging, exposing customer personal data to breach and NDPA liability.
why_it_applies: PR.DS-01 covers protection of data at rest. A.8.12 applies here because the objective is preventing leakage from organisational data stores holding personal information — distinct from the monitoring-law question at G-004.
evidence_needed: Storage inventory showing classification, encryption and immutable log sink per store; confirmed at compliance-report.json by deny STORAGE_PUBLIC for control-state.json#/storage/1
decision: Implement — funded control NF.STORAGE.RESTRICTED_PROTECTION plus addendum Clause 2

exact_locator: Pages 1-2, Evidence record: MDM dashboard screenshot — captured 18 November 2024, scope 91 devices against a current inventory of 176 company laptops
risk_statement: The only encryption evidence predates and undercounts the current estate, so the organisation cannot demonstrate current endpoint encryption coverage to an auditor or regulator.
why_it_applies: The gap is an inability to evidence the current state of the asset estate. ID.AM-01 and A.5.9 cover maintaining an accurate inventory of assets as the basis for control assurance.
evidence_needed: Current MDM export carrying metadata, reconciled against the 176-laptop inventory; confirmed at compliance-report.json by deny ENDPOINT_UNMANAGED for control-state.json#/endpoints/1 and deny ENDPOINT_EDR_UNHEALTHY for control-state.json#/endpoints/2
decision: Implement — closed by funded control NF.ENDPOINT.MANAGED_HEALTH, which requires live MDM data as evidence and excludes screenshots and historical reports

exact_locator: Page 1, Legacy AUP clause 8; page 2, Artifact reliability notes, fourth bullet (Q2 helpdesk records)
risk_statement: Incidents route through a general helpdesk with no escalation target; a 31-hour delay on a personal-data incident would breach NDPA notification expectations.
why_it_applies: RS.MA-01 covers executing incident response once an incident is declared; A.5.24 covers incident-management planning, roles and escalation paths — precisely what a helpdesk-only route lacks.
evidence_needed: Helpdesk escalation timestamps measured against the four-hour triage target; monthly review records
decision: Reduce — addendum Clause 5 sets an interim escalation route (owner: Head of IT Support and Head of Security; trigger: quarterly or immediately after any security incident); formal incident-management tooling deferred to the next funding cycle

exact_locator: Page 1, Legacy AUP clause 1; page 2, Colleague's draft mapping table, final row
risk_statement: Forced 90-day rotation encourages predictable password patterns and contradicts current authentication guidance, while the draft's justification overstates its benefit.
why_it_applies: PR.AA-01 covers identity and credential management policy; A.5.17 covers authentication information lifecycle, including how passwords are set and changed.
evidence_needed: Documented review of the rotation rule against current authentication guidance at the next policy cycle
decision: Defer — owner: Head of Security; trigger: annual policy review; no funded control consumed

exact_locator: Page 1, Stakeholder message: Engineering, VP Engineering, 9 July 2026
risk_statement: No documented joiner, mover and leaver process exists, so departed or reassigned staff can retain access and the identity control decays after go-live.
why_it_applies: Same access-lifecycle objective as G-003. Recorded separately because it concerns the automated lifecycle process Engineering offered rather than the review schedule itself.
evidence_needed: JML automation run records; service-account owner assignments feeding the identity control's decisions
decision: Reduce — adopted as an enabler of the funded identity control; company-wide JML programme deferred (owner: Head of Engineering and Head of Security; trigger: next quarterly planning cycle)
